name: Trail to La Sagra
name_original: Cordillera Subbética – Sierra y Cima de la Sagra
description: Hike the moderate 6.0-mile Trail to La Sagra, gaining 2935 feet in elevation for panoramic views from the summit.
region_intro: Hiking in Sierras Del Nordeste offers routes through a mountainous landscape characterized by prominent peaks like La Sagra. The region features varied terrain, including high-altitude trails and areas around astronomical observatories. Hikers can expect challenging ascents and panoramic views across the Sierra. Best hiking trails in Sierras Del Nordeste The most popular hiking route is Trail to La Sagra, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads towards the prominent La Sagra peak. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Sagra Summit – La Sagra Chica loop from Observatorio astronómico de La Sagra, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.6 km) path. This challenging loop offers extensive views from the summit and passes by the astronomical observatory. Local hikers also love the Ledge – La Sagra Summit loop from Observatorio astronómico de La Sagra, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through mountain terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Hiking in Sierras Del Nordeste is defined by mountain summits, high-altitude trails, and panoramic vistas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate ascents to difficult summit loops. The routes in Sierras Del Nordeste are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 230 reviews. More than 1,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Sierras Del Nordeste's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: There are over 100 hiking routes in Sierras Del Nordeste, offering a wide range of experiences. The komoot community has explored these trails more than 1,700 times, contributing to an average rating of 4.6 stars.

Q: What makes hiking in Sierras Del Nordeste unique?
A: Hiking in Sierras Del Nordeste, located on São Miguel Island in the Azores, is characterized by its dramatic coastal cliffs, lush green landscapes, and an abundance of natural features. You'll find vibrant flora, numerous waterfalls, and panoramic ocean views, especially from viewpoints like Ponta do Sossego and Ponta da Madrugada.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: Yes, Sierras Del Nordeste offers 26 easy hiking routes. These trails are perfect for a relaxed stroll, allowing you to enjoy the region's natural beauty without strenuous effort. Many trails within the Parque Natural da Ribeira dos Caldeirões are suitable for easier exploration.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are 26 difficult routes available. One such demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10320437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sagra Summit – La Sagra Chica loop from Observatorio astronómico de La Sagra&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 9.7 miles (15.6 km) with significant elevation gain, offering extensive views from the summit.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Sierras Del Nordeste are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14623051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledge – La Sagra Summit loop from Observatorio astronómico de La Sagra&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail leading through mountain terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: The region is rich in natural landmarks. You can hike to the highest point on São Miguel, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2074246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sagra Summit&lt;/a&gt;, for panoramic views. Other notable features include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1277387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Toro&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints offering stunning coastal vistas.

Q: Are there any waterfalls I can visit on a hike?
A: Sierras Del Nordeste is renowned for its waterfalls. The Parque Natural da Ribeira dos Caldeirões is a highlight, featuring multiple cascading waterfalls and old watermills. You can also visit the impressive 40-meter Salto da Farinha waterfall or hike to Salto do Prego, which tumbles into a natural pool.

Q: Are the trails in Sierras Del Nordeste suitable for families?
A: Many trails in Sierras Del Nordeste are suitable for families, especially the easier routes. The natural parks, with their lush greenery and accessible paths, provide a wonderful setting for family outings. Always check the specific trail's difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: The spring and summer months are particularly beautiful for hiking in Sierras Del Nordeste, as the region's vibrant, multicolored flowers are in full bloom. The consistent humidity also contributes to the lush landscapes year-round. Clear days offer the best panoramic views from higher elevations like Pico da Vara.

Q: Do I need a permit to hike in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: Generally, permits are not required for most hiking trails in Sierras Del Nordeste. However, if you plan to hike in specific protected areas, such as the nature reserve around Pico da Vara, it's always advisable to check local regulations for any specific access requirements or restrictions.

Q: Are there dog-friendly hiking options in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: While many outdoor areas in the Azores are welcoming to dogs, it's important to check specific trail regulations, especially within natural parks or protected reserves, as rules can vary. Always keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Sierras Del Nordeste?
A: The trails in Sierras Del Nordeste are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 230 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning panoramic views, the dramatic coastal scenery, and the diverse natural features, including the numerous waterfalls and lush forests.
tips: Top of the hill of the same name and highest point of the Altiplano Granadino and the Subbética mountain range, with 2383 meters above sea level. Its prominence (1,408 m) is striking, making it one of the most prominent peaks in the Iberian Peninsula and therefore demanding ascent as it has steep slopes. These slopes are largely covered with pine from Salta.

faqs.question: Where does the Trail to La Sagra start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the Área Recreativa Las Santas, which is a common access point for the Sierra de la Sagra. You'll find parking available there.

faqs.question: Is the Trail to La Sagra dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the natural areas of Sierra de la Sagra, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for wildlife encounters. Be prepared for rocky sections and scree that might be challenging for some dogs.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Trail to La Sagra?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. In winter, the summit is frequently snow-covered, requiring additional gear and experience. Summer can be very hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during that season.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Trail to La Sagra?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Trail to La Sagra or access the Sierra de la Sagra natural area. However, always respect local regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Trail to La Sagra?
faqs.answer: The full route, covering a distance of approximately 9.6 km with significant elevation gain, typically takes around 4 hours and 35 minutes to complete. Some circular routes might take up to 5.5 hours.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Trail to La Sagra?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite diverse. You'll encounter alternating stretches of forest road and narrower forest paths at lower elevations. As you ascend, it becomes more rugged with sections of scree and rocky bars. A notable steep, narrow passage called the "Embudo" (funnel) is just before the ridge.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the Trail to La Sagra?
faqs.answer: Due to its isolation and height, the trail offers exceptional panoramic views. From the La Sagra Summit, you can see as far as the Sierra Nevada and the agricultural areas of Murcia, Jaén, and Almería. The journey itself provides beautiful vistas of forested slopes and dramatic rock formations.

faqs.question: Is the Trail to La Sagra suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate to demanding. It involves a significant positive slope of nearly 900 meters, and some sections require sure-footedness and sturdy shoes. A good level of fitness is recommended, so it might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Water points are typically only available at the starting point, such as the Nacimiento de las Santas at the Área Recreativa Las Santas. It's crucial to carry enough water for your entire hike, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: How well-marked is the Trail to La Sagra?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally well-marked with wooden posts and cairns. You'll also find official signage, particularly along sections of the PR-A 321 trail, which overlaps with parts of this route.

faqs.question: Does the Trail to La Sagra intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino a la Sagra, the Camino a la Sagra - PR A-321, and the Las Santas - La Sagra trail, among others.
